summary: Full backend+frontend e2e for BVMCS revamp. Backend 7/7 pytest tests pass (health, chat single+multi-turn with context, leads CRUD persistence, contact form, validation). Frontend: hero CTAs/stats/nav/services(9)/work(4)/capability-tabs(5)/testimonials(3)/client-logos(24) all render. Contact form submission works (contact-success shown). Chatbot opens (via JS click), Nova replies in real-time via Claude, buying-intent triggers chatbot-lead-form, lead capture + assistant confirmation work. Found 2 issues: (1) CRITICAL - 'Made with Emergent' badge overlays the chatbot-trigger and intercepts real mouse clicks; (2) HeroScene Three.js component is imported but never rendered in JSX (data-testid='three-hero-scene' missing - hero only shows CSS .ai-orb).

ui_bugs.issue: Emergent badge (#emergent-badge) sits at bottom-right and FULLY OVERLAPS the chatbot-trigger button (both at fixed bottom-6 right-6 area, bbox 1832,992 64x64). elementFromPoint at trigger center returns the emergent-badge anchor, and a real page.mouse.click did NOT open the chatbot panel. Only JS .click() / force-click bypasses the overlay. Real users cannot open the chatbot.

HeroScene (Three.js) is imported via lazy() in App.js (lines 33-35) but is NEVER actually rendered in the Hero JSX (lines 130-220). Hero only renders a CSS-only div className='ai-orb' (data-testid='hero-orb'). PRD explicitly asks for data-testid='three-hero-scene' (Three.js canvas). CapabilitiesScene IS rendered correctly (data-testid='three-capabilities-scene' present with canvas).

action_items: Reposition chatbot-trigger so it does not collide with #emergent-badge. Options: move to bottom-left (left-6) OR raise to bottom-28/right-6 OR add right margin shift. Verify with page.elementFromPoint that the trigger is the top element at its center, and a real mouse click opens the panel.
Render <HeroScene /> inside the Hero section (wrap in <Suspense fallback={null}>) so data-testid='three-hero-scene' canvas is present. Currently only the CSS .ai-orb is shown.
Optional: keep the .ai-orb decoration but layer it behind the Three.js scene, or replace it with the Three.js orb to honor the 'AI-native 3D' aesthetic from the PRD.
critical_code_review_comments: HeroScene imported but unused — wasted lazy chunk, and PRD feature missing.

/api/chat builds transcript on every call AND passes session_id to LlmChat — emergentintegrations also manages history per session_id internally; this could double-count context. Works fine in practice but consider passing fresh session_id per call or skipping the manual transcript prepend.
Chat endpoint catches all exceptions and converts to HTTP 502 with the raw exception string — acceptable for now but consider sanitising before exposing to the client.
EmailStr is used in LeadCreate but Lead model uses plain str — fine, but verify EmailStr validation cannot produce a value that doesn't round-trip.
Z-index conflict between fixed chatbot UI (z-50) and Emergent badge (uses extremely high z-index) — chatbot must be repositioned, not just bumped above z.
seed_data_creation: Created a few TEST_-prefixed leads via /api/leads and /api/contact during pytest run; one chatbot lead 'TEST_Chat_<ts>' submitted via UI. All persisted in db.leads.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — re-run with: pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v. Chatbot trigger overlap is reproducible via JS: document.elementFromPoint(1864,1024) returns #emergent-badge instead of the chatbot trigger. To exercise the chatbot in Playwright without fixing, use page.evaluate to trigger .click() programmatically. The lead-form trigger phrase regex in App.js Chatbot.send() is /name and email|drop your name|share your name/i — Nova reliably emits 'drop your name and email' on buying-intent prompts (verified).
rca of the issue: Chatbot overlap RCA: chatbot-trigger uses 'fixed bottom-6 right-6 z-50' (Tailwind) → bottom:24px right:24px. Emergent badge is injected by the platform at the same corner with an even higher z-index and pointer events. document.elementFromPoint(triggerCenterX, triggerCenterY) returned <a id='emergent-badge'>. Reproduction: visit site → try to click the small cyan/violet circle bottom-right → click lands on Emergent badge (opens https://app.emergent.sh in new tab) instead of opening the chatbot. Mitigation: move chatbot-trigger to bottom-left (e.g., 'fixed bottom-6 left-6') OR raise it (e.g., 'fixed bottom-24 right-6') and update the chatbot-panel anchor accordingly (currently 'bottom-28 right-6').
